message:
bd-2106k: RestartTracker integrated coordinator

Introduces `RestartTracker`, a single-struct coordinator that combines
the three restart monitoring subsystems into one unit the supervisor
runtime can use directly:

- `RestartHistory` — sliding-window counting and budget evaluation
- `RestartIntensityWindow` — restarts/second intensity monitoring
- `RestartStormMonitor` — e-process-based storm detection

All timestamps are virtual (u64 nanoseconds), making the tracker
fully deterministic under lab-time scheduling.

## New types

RestartTrackerConfig
  ::from_restart(RestartConfig)          — no storm detection
  ::with_storm_detection(threshold)      — enable intensity + e-process
  ::with_storm_monitor(StormMonitorConfig)

RestartVerdict
  Allowed { attempt: u32, delay: Option<Duration> }
  Denied  { refusal: BudgetRefusal }

RestartTracker
  ::new(config) / ::from_restart_config(RestartConfig)
  .evaluate(now) -> RestartVerdict
  .evaluate_with_budget(now, &Budget) -> RestartVerdict
  .record(now)                — update window + intensity + e-process
  .recent_count(now)          — restarts in current window
  .intensity(now) -> Option   — restarts/sec (if storm enabled)
  .is_storm() / .is_intensity_storm(now)
  .storm_snapshot()           — e-process state for diagnostics
  .history()                  — underlying RestartHistory
  .reset()                    — clear all state post-escalation

## SupervisionConfig integration

Added `storm_threshold: Option<f64>` field to `SupervisionConfig` and
a `restart_tracker()` builder method that constructs a fully-wired
`RestartTracker` from the config, threading through the storm detection
threshold when set.

## Incidental Clippy fixes in bd-npn8e tests

- `i as f64` -> `f64::from(i)` (cast_lossless)
- manual `0.01 + (i as f64 * 0.1)` -> `f64::from(i).mul_add(0.1, 0.01)`
- removed unused `use crate::obligation::eprocess::AlertState` import
  in favor of fully-qualified paths at use sites

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
